linkedin.title: Most news apps are designed to keep you scrolling, not to keep you informed.
linkedin.body:
We noticed a problem in how we consume information. We spend hours reading headlines and quick summaries, but we often finish feeling like we know less about the actual context of a story than when we started. The volume of noise is high, but the signal is low.

That is why we built Deep Dive Mode for Headlinne. It is not just about getting another summary. It is designed to pull back the curtain on a story. When you toggle it on, the app connects the dots between today's news and the history that led up to it. It explains the 'why' behind the headlines so you can form your own perspective instead of just absorbing someone else's.

We believe technology should do more than just feed us content. It should help us think more clearly. True understanding comes from context, not just speed.
linkedin.cta: If you are tired of shallow news feeds, see how we are changing the experience at HEADLINNE.com.

instagram.title: Global Shifts: 5 Stories to Know

slides.headline: Global Shifts: 5 Stories to Know
slides.subtitle: From Red Sea tensions to major business deals, here is your quick briefing.

slides.headline: New Red Sea Attacks Reported
slides.explanation: The Houthi group claims it hit oil tankers while the US conducts new strikes on Iran-backed targets. This marks a significant escalation in the region's maritime trade conflict.

slides.headline: Mayor Clarifies Netanyahu Stance
slides.explanation: New York City's mayor has walked back his pledge to arrest the Israeli prime minister, saying he lacks the legal power to do so. He is now urging federal authorities to handle the ICC warrant.

slides.headline: Extortion Conviction in Hong Kong
slides.explanation: A British woman was jailed after trying to blackmail a banker for £100,000 by falsely accusing him of rape. The ruling concludes a high-profile case involving international legal systems.

slides.headline: US Tariffs Impact Brazilian Politics
slides.explanation: New US trade tariffs are ironically helping President Lula gain ground against his political rivals in Brazil. Economic pressure is weakening the opposition's strategy ahead of upcoming elections.

slides.headline: Paramount Merger Gets EU Green Light
slides.explanation: European regulators have conditionally approved the £80bn deal between Paramount and Warner Brothers. This clears a major hurdle for one of the biggest media mergers in recent history.

instagram.caption:
From international trade disputes to legal battles in major cities, there is a lot shifting in the world today. We have broken down the five most important updates to help you stay informed without the noise. Which of these stories do you think will have the biggest impact this year?

Follow @headlinne for a daily brief. More at HEADLINNE.com.

instagram.title: AI Costs and New Smart Glasses

slides.headline: AI Costs and New Smart Glasses
slides.subtitle: Swipe to see the top tech updates you need to know today.

slides.headline: Hyundai Denies Robot Talks
slides.explanation: Hyundai says its humanoid robot plans are separate from current labor negotiations. The company is clarifying its stance after the union warned that any new automation must be discussed first.

slides.headline: Samsung Reveals New Smart Glasses
slides.explanation: Samsung showed off its new smart glasses designs with a nine-hour battery life. The project is a collaboration with Google and eyewear brands like Warby Parker.

slides.headline: The US Invests $5B in AI
slides.explanation: The US government is launching a $5 billion initiative to advance scientific research using AI. Multiple federal agencies are pooling resources to support this interdisciplinary effort.

slides.headline: Google's Massive AI Spending
slides.explanation: Google expects to spend nearly $190 billion on AI investments this year alone. This massive cost shows just how expensive the race for AI dominance has become.

slides.headline: Xbox Games Coming to PCs
slides.explanation: Microsoft is finally bringing classic Xbox titles to Windows PCs. You can even run the first four compatible games on hardware as old as 11 years.

instagram.caption:
From massive AI investments to the return of classic gaming, here is what is happening in tech today. We are breaking down the stories that actually matter without the noise. Which of these updates are you most interested in?

Follow @headlinne for a daily brief. More at HEADLINNE.com.
